description: As a Project Finance Analyst, determine the maximum allowable capital cost (target cost) for a 50 MW single-axis tracking solar project near Phoenix, AZ to achieve an 8.5% after-tax Internal Rate of Return (IRR). Using PySAM with the 'PVWattsSingleOwner' configuration, model the system with a 50,000 kW capacity, array type 2, DC/AC ratio of 1.3, and 14.0% losses. Use the Phoenix TMY weather file found via the path in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt. Set financial parameters to a PPA price of 0.04, fixed O&M of 0, and capacity-based O&M of 15. Implement an optimization to find the 'total_installed_cost' that results in an IRR between 8.45% and 8.55%. Save the results, including the target cost per watt, to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/target_cost_result.json with the keys: target_installed_cost_usd, target_cost_per_watt_usd, achieved_irr_pct, annual_energy_kwh, and weather_file.
